evals.query: Set up Logback logging in my Kora service. What dependency and application module do I need?
expected_behavior: Adds implementation "ru.tinkoff.kora:logging-logback" with the version managed by the kora-parent BOM (no inline version)
Includes the mandatory annotation processor (annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors", or ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors" for Kotlin)
Declares @KoraApp interface Application extends LogbackModule
Bootstraps via KoraApplication.run(ApplicationGraph::graph)
Does not use foreign-framework annotations, a foreign application-bootstrap annotation, or field injection

evals.query: How do I set per-package log levels for my Kora app without editing logback.xml for each one?
expected_behavior: Uses the logging.levels block in the Kora config (HOCON or YAML)
Shows keys like "ROOT", "ru.tinkoff.kora", and the application package mapped to levels
Explains that logback.xml only needs the root level and appenders; per-package levels come from config
Does not invent a foreign application.properties logging.level.* scheme

evals.query: Wrap my console appender in a non-blocking async appender in logback.xml for a Kora service.
expected_behavior: Uses ru.tinkoff.kora.logging.logback.KoraAsyncAppender, not Logback's stock AsyncAppender
Explains it extends AsyncAppenderBase and uses queueSize / discardingThreshold (not bufferSize / flushThreshold)
Notes KoraAsyncAppender propagates Kora structured MDC to the async record
References the inner appender via <appender-ref>
Does not invent a bufferSize or flushThreshold element

evals.query: Emit JSON logs from my Kora service for our ELK stack.
expected_behavior: Adds the third-party net.logstash.logback:logstash-logback-encoder dependency
Configures a ConsoleAppender with the LogstashEncoder, wrapped by KoraAsyncAppender
Notes that Kora's logging-logback ships ConsoleTextRecordEncoder for text output, so JSON requires the Logstash encoder
Optionally shows customFields and MDC key inclusion
Does not claim Kora has a built-in JSON encoder class

evals.query: Attach a structured user object to a log line in my Kora service so it shows up as JSON fields.
expected_behavior: Uses ru.tinkoff.kora.logging.common.arg.StructuredArgument with arg(...) or marker(...)
Prefers the typed overloads (String/Integer/Long/Boolean/Map<String,String>) or a writer lambda over the Jackson JsonGenerator
Distinguishes parameter (interpolated in the message) from marker (metadata only)
Uses parameterized logging (log.info("... {}", arg)), never string concatenation
Does not call a non-existent two-argument gen.writeString(name, value)

evals.query: Add a traceId to every log line within a request scope in my Kora service.
expected_behavior: Uses ru.tinkoff.kora.logging.common.MDC.put / remove for structured, async-safe context
Explains KoraAsyncAppender propagates Kora MDC, and that org.slf4j.MDC (with putCloseable) is the string-only alternative
Warns not to import both MDC classes in the same file
Removes/clears keys after use
Does not rely on a foreign RequestContextHolder or thread-local filter idiom

evals.query: Populate MDC from incoming HTTP request headers for all controllers in my Kora service.
expected_behavior: Implements ru.tinkoff.kora.http.server.common.HttpServerInterceptor
Uses the real signature CompletionStage<HttpServerResponse> intercept(Context, HttpServerRequest, InterceptChain) and returns chain.process(context, request)
Reads a header via request.headers().getFirst(name)
Applies to all controllers with @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) and registers it as a @Component
Does not use a fabricated HttpServerCall, call.proceed, or request.header(...).orElse(...)

evals.query: Log method arguments and result automatically on a service method, and add an MDC key, without writing log statements.
expected_behavior: Uses the @Log aspect (or @Log.in / @Log.out) from ru.tinkoff.kora.logging.common.annotation
Uses @Mdc to add MDC keys from parameters or generated ${...} values, and @Log.off to exclude secrets
Notes the aspects come from logging-common and require the Kora annotation processor
Notes the class must be non-final (Java) or open (Kotlin) for aspects to apply
Does not use foreign-framework AOP, @Around advice, or AspectJ

evals.query: My Kora HTTP server and database produce no telemetry log lines. How do I turn them on?
expected_behavior: Explains module telemetry logging is disabled by default and is enabled per module
Sets httpServer.telemetry.logging.enabled = true and db.telemetry.logging.enabled = true
Notes clients are configured per named client (e.g. grpcClient.SomeService.telemetry.logging.enabled)
Does not invent a per-module telemetry.logging.level key
